This holistic support includes: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Institutional Capacity Building Organisations receive guidance on improving governance structures, operational systems, and program management practices. Technical Expertise SSF provides expert insights and mentorship to help organisations refine training programs, improve athlete development pathways, and implement effective sports management practices. Child Safeguarding Implementation One of the program s key priorities is ensuring safe sporting environments. SSF supports partners in adopting and implementing child safeguarding policies that protect young athletes. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Monitoring and Evaluation Support Organisations are encouraged to integrate monitoring and evaluation frameworks to track progress, measure outcomes, and strengthen program effectiveness. Health and Well-Being Integration SSF promotes the integration of health awareness initiatives within sports programs, particularly those addressing issues affecting girl athletes. Through these combined resources, the program ensures that organisations develop strong institutional foundations capable of sustaining long-term impact. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); SSF s Track Record and Demonstrated Impact The Simply Sport Foundation has already demonstrated the potential of its partnership model through previous yearly grant initiatives. Over the years, SSF has collaborated with numerous grassroots sports organisations across India, helping them improve program quality and expand opportunities for young athletes. Key impact highlights from past partnerships include: Implementation of child safeguarding policies across partner organisations to strengthen athlete protection. Organisation of child safeguarding workshops and awareness sessions for coaches, staff, and stakeholders. Strengthened institutional practices within grassroots sports organisations. Increased participation among girl athletes through targeted health and awareness programs. These achievements demonstrate the effectiveness of SSF s approach in creating safer, more inclusive sports environments while empowering grassroots organisations to grow sustainably. Voices from Past Partner Organisations Feedback from previous partner organisations illustrates the real-world impact of SSF s support. One partner organisation highlighted how the grant enabled access to better sports infrastructure and athlete development resources: Grants of this nature, designed to support initiatives across the spectrum from grassroots to elite youth, are virtually non-existent in India. This collaboration has enabled our residential hockey program to access quality equipment and competitive exposure opportunities. It has also opened doors to essential sports science resources that will meaningfully enhance the performance of these young athletes at elite levels. Another organisation reported a significant increase in girls participation after health education initiatives supported by SSF: The workshop on female athletes menstrual health has motivated many girls to take up sports. The awareness about menstrual cycles and menstrual health resulted in a 30% increase in girls participation in sports . These testimonials highlight the transformative potential of a partnership model that combines funding, education, and institutional support. The Three-Year Partnership Framework The SSF program is structured as a phased development model , ensuring that organisations grow gradually and sustainably over the three-year period. Each year builds upon the progress of the previous one: Year 1: Foundation Building Organisations focus on establishing strong program structures, improving training systems, and adopting safeguarding policies. Year 2: Program Strengthening Partners refine their sports programs, improve athlete development pathways, and expand participation. Year 3: Sustainability and Scaling By the final year, organisations are expected to operate with stronger institutional systems and the capacity to sustain programs independently. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); This gradual progression ensures that the partnership leads to long-term organisational resilience rather than short-term program expansion. Expected Outcomes of the Program The SSF Multi-Year Grant Partnership Program aims to achieve several measurable outcomes that directly impact the lives of girl athletes. Key targets include: 20 30% reduction in dropout rates among girl athletes enrolled in partner programs. Institutionalisation of child safeguarding practices as a routine part of sports operations. Engagement of senior girl athletes as mentors, coaches, and role models for younger participants. Development of replicable program models that can inspire similar initiatives across the country. These outcomes reflect SSF s broader mission of ensuring that sport becomes a lasting and empowering space for girls. Eligibility Criteria for Applicant Organisations To ensure that selected organisations can fully benefit from the program, SSF has established a set of eligibility criteria that applicants must meet to progress to the second round of the selection process. Eligible organisations must: Be legally registered as a Trust, Society, or Section 8 Company. Have been operational for at least three years with ongoing sports activities. Conduct a minimum of three structured weekly training sessions for girl athletes. Have access to safe and consistent training infrastructure . Employ at least one designated coach responsible for girls training programs . Be located in rural or semi-urban areas . Demonstrate active involvement of founders or senior leadership in daily operations. Have existing girl athlete enrolment or a clear plan for expansion . Show willingness to implement child safeguarding practices and maintain program records . Commit to monitoring, evaluation, health integration, and youth development principles . Organisations that meet these requirements will be eligible to proceed to the next stage of the application process. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(139); }); Application Process and Timeline The SSF Multi-Year Grant Partnership Program uses a two-round selection process designed to ensure both fairness and thorough evaluation while remaining manageable for grassroots organisations. Key Dates Applications Open: March 11, 2026 Application Deadline: April 10, 2026 Results of Round 1: Within four weeks after the deadline Partnership Awards Announced: By May 2026 Round 1 Eligibility Screening Applicants submit basic organisational and program information through the initial application form. This stage determines whether organisations meet the foundational criteria. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(140); }); Round 2 Comprehensive Evaluation Shortlisted organisations are invited to submit detailed proposals including: Program proposal Budget plan Monitoring and evaluation framework Participation in a panel interview with SSF s expert review committee This two-stage process allows SSF to identify organisations that are best positioned to create meaningful impact through the partnership. How to Apply Interested organisations can access the Round 1 Application Form starting March 11, 2026 , and must submit their applications before April 10, 2026 . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(141); }); Since program capacity is limited, SSF strongly encourages eligible organisations to apply early to increase their chances of consideration. Organisations that are unsure about their eligibility are encouraged to contact the SSF team for clarification and guidance before submitting an application. Building a Future Where Girls Thrive in Sport The Simply Sport Foundation s Multi-Year Grant Partnership Program represents a powerful commitment to strengthening grassroots sports and expanding opportunities for girls across India. By combining financial investment, institutional support, and long-term collaboration , the program empowers sports organisations to create environments where girls can learn, compete, and grow confidently. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(142); }); At its core, the initiative reflects a simple yet transformative vision: Sport should be a lasting home for every girl. For grassroots sports organisations dedicated to that mission, the SSF Multi-Year Grant Partnership Program 2026 offers not just funding but a pathway to sustainable impact and meaningful change. title: Sigrid Jusélius Foundation Grants 2026: Major Funding Opportunities for Medical Researchers at Every Career Stage
summary: Funding is one of the most critical factors enabling groundbreaking scientific discoveries and sustaining long-term research careers. For decades, the Sigrid Jus lius Foundation has been one of the most influential supporters of medical research in Finland, providing significant funding opportunities to researchers working at various stages of their academic and professional journeys. Through its diverse portfolio of grants, fellowships, and research positions, the foundation aims to promote high-quality medical research , strengthen research careers, and advance scientific knowledge that contributes to better healthcare outcomes. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The foundation primarily supports research conducted in Finland, while also encouraging international collaboration by funding research placements abroad and inviting foreign scholars to Finnish institutions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Notably, at least 75 percent of the grants awarded by the foundation go to Finnish researchers , reinforcing the country s research ecosystem while also facilitating global partnerships. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Several funding opportunities are currently open for applications in 2026 , particularly for researchers seeking fellowships or senior research placements abroad. About the Sigrid Jus lius Foundation The Sigrid Jus lius Foundation is dedicated to supporting top-level medical research in Finland . Its funding programs are designed to promote scientific excellence, enhance international collaboration, and support researchers throughout their careers from postdoctoral researchers to senior academics. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); By providing financial support for research projects, fellowships, and research positions, the foundation enables scientists to pursue innovative research initiatives that may lead to major advancements in medicine and public health. In addition to funding research careers, the foundation also supports medical symposiums , providing platforms for scientific exchange, collaboration, and dissemination of new knowledge. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Available Grant Opportunities The foundation offers several types of funding opportunities tailored to different stages of the research career. These grants support both research conducted in Finland and research placements abroad , helping researchers expand their expertise and collaborate internationally. Below are the primary funding opportunities available through the foundation. Fellowship (1 3 Years) The Fellowship is designed for researchers who have completed their doctoral studies and wish to pursue advanced medical research abroad. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); This fellowship enables early-career researchers to develop their expertise, strengthen international collaboration, and contribute to innovative research projects at leading research institutions worldwide. Key Details Duration: 1 3 years Target group: Researchers who have defended their doctoral thesis Purpose: Medical research abroad ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Application Period 15 March 15 April 2026 This opportunity is particularly valuable for postdoctoral researchers seeking to expand their international research networks and gain experience in new research environments. Senior Fellowship (6 12 Months) The Senior Fellowship supports experienced researchers who wish to conduct advanced medical research abroad for a shorter duration. This funding allows senior-level researchers to collaborate with international research institutions, contribute to high-impact projects, and strengthen international partnerships. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Key Details Duration: 6 12 months Target group: Senior-level researchers Purpose: Medical research abroad Application Period 15 March 15 April 2026 The Senior Fellowship is ideal for established researchers seeking to deepen their expertise or contribute to collaborative global research initiatives. Visiting Professor Grant (3 12 Months) The Visiting Professor Grant supports foreign senior researchers who wish to work at Finnish research institutes . This initiative encourages knowledge exchange between international scholars and Finnish research institutions, strengthening Finland s position as a hub for cutting-edge medical research. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Key Details Duration: 3 12 months Target group: Foreign senior researchers Purpose: Conduct research at Finnish institutions Application Period 1 November 30 November 2026 1 3 Year Research Grants These grants are designed to support research projects led by experienced scientists and emerging research leaders. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Key Details Duration: 1 3 years Target group: Senior researchers Young group leaders Application Period 1 November 30 November 2026 These grants provide funding that allows researchers to pursue long-term medical research initiatives that contribute to scientific innovation. Large Research Grant (3 5 Years) The Large Research Grant is intended for highly accomplished academic researchers who lead major research initiatives. Target Group Professors Highly merited adjunct professors (docents) This funding supports larger-scale research projects with significant scientific impact. Senior Researcher Position (4 Years) The Senior Researcher Grant supports independent medical researchers who have already established themselves in their field. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Key Details Duration: 4 years Target group: Adjunct professors (docents) Independent medical researchers with equivalent qualifications Application Period 15 September 15 October 2026 Medical Professorship (5 Years) The foundation also provides funding to support medical professorships , helping universities and research institutions maintain world-class academic leadership in medical science. Key Details Duration: 5 years Purpose: Financing medical professorship positions Medical Symposium Funding In addition to research grants and fellowships, the Sigrid Jus lius Foundation also supports medical symposiums that promote knowledge exchange among researchers. These symposium grants are awarded every two years . Application Period 15 September 15 October 2025 Application Process All grant applications must be submitted through the foundation s electronic application system during the designated application periods. Applicants should carefully review the specific instructions provided on each grant page before submitting their proposal. In most cases, researchers can receive only one grant at a time , ensuring fair distribution of funding opportunities among applicants. Applicants are encouraged to prepare their research proposals well in advance to meet the application deadlines. Annual Grant Application Timeline The Sigrid Jus lius Foundation organizes its funding opportunities throughout the year according to a structured annual application cycle . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); Key funding rounds occur in: March April (Fellowships and Senior Fellowships) September October (Senior Researcher Grants and Symposium Funding) November (Visiting Professor Grants and 1 3 Year Research Grants) This structured timeline allows researchers to plan their applications according to their career stage and research needs. Why Apply for Sigrid Jus lius Foundation Funding? Receiving a grant from the Sigrid Jus lius Foundation is widely recognized as a prestigious achievement within the medical research community. The foundation s grants provide: Significant financial support for high-quality research Opportunities for international collaboration Career advancement for emerging and established researchers Access to leading research institutions worldwide Through these grants, the foundation continues to strengthen Finland s role in global medical research while fostering scientific innovation that benefits society.
